description: Add Firestore real-time listeners (onSnapshot) to KDB layer so that when any user saves/updates data, ALL other connected users automatically receive the update without manual refresh.
steps: 1. Import onSnapshot from Firebase Firestore module in initKFirebase() and add it to the kfs object.
2. Add a subscribeCollection(col, callback) method to KDB that sets up an onSnapshot listener on a collection.
3. Add startRealtimeSync() function that subscribes to critical collections (jurnal, permohonan, danamasuk, inventori_atk, k_settings) after login.
4. When a snapshot update arrives from another user, update the localStorage cache AND re-render the current active section using navigate(currentSection).
5. Use _recentSaves tracking to skip self-triggered snapshot changes (avoid infinite loops).
6. Add stopRealtimeSync() to unsubscribe all listeners on logout.
7. Call startRealtimeSync() from buildApp() after login, call stopRealtimeSync() from doLogout().
acceptance_criteria: onSnapshot is imported and available in the kfs object
After login, critical collections are subscribed
Snapshot updates from other users refresh the current view
Self-triggered changes do not cause re-renders (using _recentSaves)
node -c keuangan-firebase.js and node -c keuangan-app.js pass without errors
findings: Successfully implemented onSnapshot real-time listeners. The solution: (1) Added fm.onSnapshot to the kfs object in initKFirebase(). (2) Created _kSubscribeCollection() that sets up onSnapshot on each collection with self-change detection using KDB._recentSaves (10s window). (3) Created startRealtimeSync() subscribing to jurnal, permohonan, danamasuk, inventori_atk, and settings collections. (4) Created stopRealtimeSync() to clean up listeners on logout. (5) Integrated into buildApp() and doLogout(). (6) The initial snapshot is skipped to avoid redundant re-renders at login. (7) Dirty item merging is applied in the snapshot handler to protect local writes. Syntax validation passed (node -c) for both files.
